granblueRelinkCodes.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink Codes, Relink Pack, and Redemption Rules
granblueRelinkCodes.subtitle: Use this guide to separate real official rewards from fake code lists, and to understand how PlayStation, Steam, and Endless Ragnarok bonuses differ.
granblueRelinkCodes.intro: Granblue Fantasy: Relink does not use public universal gift codes. Real code-related rewards come from official purchases, platform vouchers, eligible physical copies, eligible digital editions, or official add-ons. The most important restriction is platform-based: the original Relink Pack serial-code bonus is for PlayStation versions and is not included with Steam versions.

items.title: Granblue Special Item Set: Relink Pack
appliesTo: Eligible physical PlayStation copies of Granblue Fantasy: Relink
Eligible PlayStation digital purchases made during the official inclusion period
notIncludedOn: Steam versions of Granblue Fantasy: Relink
PlayStation digital purchases made after the original inclusion period ended
items.rewardSummary: A bonus for the browser and mobile game Granblue Fantasy, not an item pack used directly inside Relink.
includedRewards: Choice of one progression set: Eternals' Transcendence Set or Evokers' Domain Set
eligibilityNotes: A Granblue Fantasy browser or mobile game account is required.
Each Relink Pack can be redeemed once on a single Granblue Fantasy account.
For PlayStation digital purchases, the Relink Pack is tied to the purchasing PlayStation Network account instead of being issued as a separate printed voucher.
Cygames extended the Relink Pack redemption deadline beyond the original December 31, 2024 date.

items.title: How to Redeem the Relink Pack
steps: On PlayStation, sign in to the PlayStation Network account that owns the eligible copy or voucher.
For a printed voucher, open PlayStation Store and use Redeem Code to enter the 12-digit PlayStation voucher code.
Launch Granblue Fantasy: Relink and open Serial Code from the title screen or from Siero's Knickknack Shack.
Select Show Serial Code to display the serial code for Granblue Fantasy.
Open Granblue Fantasy in a browser or supported mobile environment and enter the serial code on the in-game Serial Codes screen.
Claim the delivered items from the Crate in Granblue Fantasy.
platformRules: PlayStation Store voucher codes are region and account bound.
Voucher codes are one-time-use and cannot be transferred after redemption.
The Relink Pack serial code cannot be entered through the iOS version of Granblue Fantasy; use a browser instead.
Lost serial codes are not reissued.

items.title: Original Relink Pre-Order Bonuses
includedRewards: Early Access on eligible PlayStation editions
items.availability: The original pre-order bonus period for Granblue Fantasy: Relink has ended.
platformRules: Early Access was not included with the Steam version.
Add-on and bonus items are claimed in Relink through Siero's Knickknack Shack under Claim Add-Ons.

items.title: Endless Ragnarok Purchase Bonuses
appliesTo: Granblue Fantasy: Relink - Endless Ragnarok full editions
Granblue Fantasy: Relink - Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit editions
includedRewards: Full Endless Ragnarok editions: Guts III and Autorevive III Sigil Set
Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit editions: Supplementary Damage V and War Elemental Sigil Set
platformRules: The Upgrade Kit requires ownership of the base game on the same platform.
Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and PlayStation 4 purchase-bonus eligibility runs through July 8, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. local time.
Steam purchase-bonus eligibility runs through August 31, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. local time.
Each early purchase bonus can be claimed once per user account.

items.title: Fake Online Gift-Code Lists
whatToIgnore: Public lists claiming to offer free crystals, rupies, SSR tickets, weapon packs, or universal Relink gift codes
Surveys or login pages that ask for PlayStation Network, Steam, Nintendo, Cygames, or Granblue Fantasy account credentials
items.safeRule: Use only codes and bonuses distributed through official product pages, official stores, platform stores, or official Cygames announcements.

items.title: Third-Party Serial-Code Buying and Selling
items.officialRule: Cygames states that Relink Pack serial-code trading, resale, or exchange through auctions or third-party services is strictly prohibited.
practicalRisks: A code can already be used before delivery.
A code can be region-locked or tied to a different platform account.
A purchased code can violate official serial-code rules.
Support will not reissue lost or invalid third-party codes.
items.safeRule: Buy eligible editions or add-ons from official stores instead of third-party serial-code sellers.

granblueRelinkTierList.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink Character Tier List
granblueRelinkTierList.subtitle: A practical role-based tier list built around beginner comfort, AI teammate value, co-op usefulness, late-game damage, and survivability.
granblueRelinkTierList.intro: Cygames publishes character profiles, combat systems, updates, and expansion content, but it does not publish an official strength ranking. This tier list is a site editorial ranking designed for players choosing who to build first, who to assign as AI teammates, and who to bring into co-op or late-game quests.

items.label: Top all-around picks
items.summary: Strong choices for long-term investment because they combine reliable damage, strong team value, survivability, or excellent AI consistency.

characters.name: Cagliostro
characters.role: Support caster and alchemy damage dealer
characters.note: A flexible support pick with healing, buffs, and steady ranged pressure, making her valuable in solo parties and online play.

characters.name: Eugen
characters.note: A safe ranged attacker who performs well as an AI teammate and helps keep damage uptime against mobile bosses.

characters.name: Lancelot
characters.note: Fast attacks, easy movement, and strong uptime make him one of the most comfortable melee characters for new and experienced players.

characters.name: Charlotta
characters.note: Short reach is balanced by excellent mobility, constant pressure, and simple execution.

characters.name: Sandalphon
characters.note: A powerful post-launch character with strong payoff once his mechanics are understood.

characters.name: Narmaya
characters.note: Excellent damage ceiling with stance management; best for players who enjoy active melee timing.

items.label: Very strong specialists
items.summary: Excellent characters that perform best when their timing, range, or character-specific mechanics are used properly.

characters.name: Vane
characters.note: A sturdy frontliner whose defensive tools are especially useful in difficult co-op quests.

characters.name: Percival
characters.note: Strong burst damage with deliberate charge timing; rewards players who know boss openings.

characters.name: Zeta
characters.note: Great aerial pressure and satisfying timing windows, with more execution demand than simpler melee picks.

characters.name: Siegfried
characters.note: Durable and powerful when attack timing is maintained, but less forgiving than faster characters.

characters.name: Id
characters.note: A strong story character with a clear power fantasy and solid performance after his kit opens up.

characters.name: Seofon
characters.note: A high-ceiling DLC-era character built around sword pressure and strong offensive payoff.

characters.name: Tweyen
characters.note: A ranged Eternal who brings strong coverage and safe uptime once unlocked or purchased.

characters.name: Beatrix
characters.note: Cycles offensive, defensive, and recovery self-buffs, giving her strong flexibility in expansion content.

characters.name: Eustace
characters.note: A ranged expansion character suited to players who prefer steady distance control.

characters.name: Captain
characters.note: A reliable first investment with simple skills, party utility, and story availability from the start.

items.label: Good but more situational
items.summary: Useful characters with clear strengths, but they need more setup, more careful play, or a team that supports their preferred combat pattern.

characters.name: Katalina
characters.note: A safe early-game choice with defensive value, though her overall output is less explosive than top melee picks.

characters.name: Io
characters.note: Strong magic damage when charge windows are respected, but boss movement can interrupt her best rhythm.

characters.name: Rackam
characters.note: Easy to use and safe at range, but generally less valuable than the highest-performing ranged options.

characters.name: Ferry
characters.note: Strong control and reach, with a playstyle that depends on managing spacing and summoned pets.

characters.name: Rosetta
characters.note: Excellent for players who understand placement, but less straightforward in fast-moving fights.

characters.name: Vaseraga
characters.note: Durable and hard-hitting, but slow animations make him less beginner-friendly.

characters.name: Yodarha
characters.note: Capable in practiced hands, but less universal than the easiest high-tier melee picks.

characters.name: Maglielle
characters.note: An expansion character with stylish blade-based pressure and a more specialized learning curve.

characters.name: Fraux
characters.note: A powerful expansion pick for players who want a more character-specific playstyle.

characters.name: Fediel
characters.note: A Six Dragons character with strong theme and late-game potential, best treated as a focused investment.

characters.name: Gallanza
characters.note: A powerhouse with wide attack range, suited to players who like heavy melee commitment.

items.label: Niche investment
items.summary: Playable and usable, but less efficient as a first major investment compared with easier or more broadly valuable characters.

characters.name: Ghandagoza
characters.note: Can hit hard, but his slow and commitment-heavy style is demanding against mobile bosses.

granblueRelinkBeginnerGuide.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink Beginner Guide
granblueRelinkBeginnerGuide.subtitle: A clean starting path for new players: story first, simple combat habits, sensible upgrades, and no early-game resource panic.
granblueRelinkBeginnerGuide.intro: Relink is built around a four-person party, real-time action combat, story chapters, and repeatable quests. The smoothest start is to learn one main character, let AI teammates cover support and ranged damage, and avoid dumping rare materials into every shiny weapon like the sky realm is running a financial literacy experiment.

items.title: Start with the story and learn the core menus
items.goal: Unlock basic systems before worrying about builds.
actions: Play through the opening story chapters instead of grinding immediately.
Use the main menu to review characters, weapons, skills, sigils, and quests.
Read tutorial prompts when new battle systems unlock.
avoid: Do not judge a character before their core skills and weapon upgrades are available.
Do not spend rare materials just because a menu shows an upgrade button.
items.whyItMatters: Story progress unlocks more characters, systems, quests, town services, and better upgrade paths.

items.title: Pick one comfortable main character first
items.goal: Build muscle memory instead of spreading resources across the whole roster.
actions: Use Captain, Lancelot, Charlotta, Eugen, or Katalina for an easy early learning curve.
Test characters in quests before committing major materials.
Keep one main character slightly ahead in weapon level, skills, and sigils.
avoid: Do not try to master the entire roster in the first few hours.
Do not force a technical character if basic dodging, guarding, and skill timing still feel messy.
items.whyItMatters: A focused main character clears story and early quests faster, which unlocks better rewards for the rest of the party.

items.title: Learn the basic battle loop
items.goal: Understand how damage windows work before chasing advanced builds.
actions: Use normal attacks to build pressure and create openings.
Use character skills when the boss is still, stunned, or locked in a long animation.
Watch the stun gauge and trigger Link Attacks when available.
Chain Skybound Arts with teammates to create Chain Bursts.
Guard or dodge instead of attacking through every boss animation.
avoid: Do not spend every skill the moment it comes off cooldown.
Do not ignore Link Attacks and Skybound Arts; they are core party-damage tools.
items.whyItMatters: Relink rewards uptime, timing, and party chains more than button mashing.

items.title: Build a balanced four-person party
items.goal: Let AI teammates cover jobs your main character does not handle well.
actions: Run one main damage dealer controlled by you.
Add one durable melee or defensive character for stability.
Add one ranged attacker for consistent boss uptime.
Add one support-leaning character with healing, buffs, or safety tools.
avoid: Do not build four fragile melee attackers as a first party.
Do not leave AI teammates under-leveled while only upgrading your main character.
items.whyItMatters: AI allies can contribute strong damage, revives, link attacks, and Skybound Arts when they are properly equipped.

items.title: Spend early Mastery Points on simple value
items.goal: Buy upgrades that help immediately in every quest.
actions: Prioritize basic offense, health, skill access, and practical survivability.
Unlock useful character skills before polishing small stat nodes.
Keep your main party's Mastery growth reasonably even.
avoid: Do not over-optimize tiny stat routes during the story.
Do not ignore defensive upgrades if bosses are knocking characters down repeatedly.
items.whyItMatters: Early Mastery choices should make fights safer and faster, not turn the first playthrough into a spreadsheet with sword noises.

items.title: Upgrade weapons and sigils with restraint
items.goal: Stay strong enough for current quests while saving rare materials for better gear.
actions: Keep your active party's equipped weapons upgraded enough to meet quest difficulty.
Use sigils that clearly match your needs: attack, health, cooldown help, stun, survival, or character-specific value.
Replace weak early sigils as stronger versions drop from quests.
avoid: Do not fully invest in every weapon as soon as it appears.
Do not hoard so hard that your party falls behind basic quest requirements.
items.whyItMatters: Relink gives many upgrade paths, and careful spending prevents early material shortages.

items.title: Use town services between quests
items.goal: Turn quest rewards into steady progression.
actions: Visit Siero's Knickknack Shack to claim add-ons, trade items, and manage useful services.
Check quest rewards before farming so you know what a mission actually drops.
Upgrade, equip, and review party members after major story beats.
avoid: Do not leave bonus items unclaimed if you own eligible add-ons.
Do not farm random quests without checking whether they drop the material you need.
items.whyItMatters: Town management is where most character growth happens between battles.

items.title: After the story, climb quest ranks gradually
items.goal: Move from casual clearing into endgame preparation.
actions: Clear available quest tiers in order instead of jumping straight into the hardest missions.
Strengthen your main party before chasing perfect late-game sigils.
Use co-op when a quest is slow or inconsistent with AI teammates.
Learn boss patterns because higher quest ranks punish careless revives and missed damage windows.
avoid: Do not copy an endgame build before unlocking the systems and materials that make it work.
Do not treat one failed high-rank quest as a character problem; it is often a gear, timing, or pattern-knowledge problem.
items.whyItMatters: The post-story game is built around repeatable quests, stronger gear, tighter execution, and better party planning.

granblueRelinkEndlessRagnarok.title: Granblue Fantasy: Relink - Endless Ragnarok
granblueRelinkEndlessRagnarok.subtitle: A full guide to the expansion's release, editions, upgrade kits, demo, bonuses, new story, characters, Chaos quests, Conflux, summons, and crossplay.
granblueRelinkEndlessRagnarok.intro: Endless Ragnarok is the major expansion for Granblue Fantasy: Relink. It adds a new story chapter, six playable characters, new systems, harder quests, solo endgame content, and cross-platform online play across Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation, and Steam.

items.title: Release Date and Platforms
details: Granblue Fantasy: Relink - Endless Ragnarok launches on July 9, 2026 according to Cygames' official announcement.
The expansion supports Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam.
The Steam product page lists the Upgrade Kit release date as July 8, 2026, reflecting the Steam store timing.
The game supports 1 player offline and 1 to 4 players online.

items.title: Which Edition to Buy
details: New players can buy Granblue Fantasy: Relink - Endless Ragnarok as a full game package.
Existing owners of the base game can buy the Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit.
The Upgrade Kit requires the base game on the same platform.
Standard and Special versions are available depending on platform and format.

items.title: Standard vs Special Edition Contents
details: Standard full editions include the base game and Endless Ragnarok content.
Standard Upgrade Kit editions add Endless Ragnarok content for players who already own the base game.
Special full editions include the Endless Ragnarok Special Pack.
Special full editions and Special Upgrade Kit editions include the DLC Collection.

items.title: Pre-Order and Early Purchase Bonuses
details: Full Endless Ragnarok editions include the Guts III and Autorevive III Sigil Set as the purchase bonus.
Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit editions include the Supplementary Damage V and War Elemental Sigil Set as the purchase bonus.
Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, and PlayStation 4 bonuses are available for purchases through July 8, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. local time.
Steam bonuses are available for purchases through August 31, 2026 at 11:59 p.m. local time.
Each bonus can be claimed once per user account.

items.title: Demo Content
details: The Endless Ragnarok demo became available on June 18, 2026.
The PlayStation demo includes Story Mode, Quest Mode, and Tutorial Mode.
Quest Mode includes four quests in total.
Clearing the first three demo quests unlocks Sephira's Sanguine Glimmer, a quest that allows access to Summons.
Each demo quest grants rewards that can be redeemed in the full game after release.
Demo save data does not carry over to the full version.

items.title: New Story, Bosses, and Chaos Quests
details: Endless Ragnarok adds a new story arc after the base Relink adventure.
The expansion introduces new skybound threats including Beelzebub and The World.
Chaos is a new high-difficulty quest tier designed as the expansion's most demanding challenge.
Chaos quests feature ragnalia and new boss mechanics, including EX Burst attacks.

items.title: Six New Playable Characters
details: Gallanza joins as a heavy spear-axe powerhouse with wide attack range.
Maglielle joins as a magic-blade wielder and leader of the Sword Veil Fellowship.
Beatrix joins as a Society fighter who cycles offensive, defensive, and recovery self-buffs.
Eustace joins as a Society marksman wielding the seal weapon Flamek Thunder.
Fraux joins as a pactbearer to The Devil.
Fediel joins as one of the Six Dragons with dominion over darkness.

items.title: Summons and Primal Bursts
details: Endless Ragnarok adds Summons as a new battle mechanic.
Summons can be called during battle and have attack skills plus support abilities.
The player becomes invincible while directly controlling a summon.
Proto Bahamut and Excavallion can unleash Primal Bursts after specific battle conditions are met and all four party members use Skybound Arts.

items.title: The Conflux Solo Mode
details: Conflux is a solo-exclusive mode built around sequential zones, combat objectives, and temporary perks.
Players choose one of three temporary perks during a run.
Conflux objectives and perk choices vary between runs.
Rewards include materials and high-tier gear for character and weapon enhancement.

items.title: Crossplay and Local Play
details: Endless Ragnarok adds cross-platform online play across Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam.
Nintendo Switch 2 supports local communication with up to four players.
Online play can require the relevant platform's online membership service.
GameShare is not available during Nintendo Switch 2 local communication.

granblueRelinkCharacters.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Characters
granblueRelinkCharacters.subtitle: All playable, update, and Endless Ragnarok characters in one roster breakdown.
granblueRelinkCharacters.intro: Browse the main launch roster, free update characters, and the new playable characters added with Endless Ragnarok. Each card covers role, source, playable status, and who should try them first.

items.name: Main Character (Gran / Djeeta)
items.role: Flexible sword user and party anchor
items.unlockOrAccess: Available as the player character, with Gran or Djeeta selected as the Captain.
items.bestFor: Players who want a balanced starter character, easy party access, and a flexible build path.

items.name: Katalina
items.role: Saber wielder with defensive and support utility
items.unlockOrAccess: Available through the base story roster.
items.bestFor: Players who want a steady melee character with protection, sustain, and team-friendly tools.

items.name: Rackam
items.unlockOrAccess: Available through the base story roster.
items.bestFor: Players who prefer ranged pressure, safer positioning, and consistent damage uptime.

items.name: Io
items.role: Mage with charged spells and healing support
items.unlockOrAccess: Available through the base story roster.
items.bestFor: Players who like spellcasting, burst windows, and helping the party recover.

items.name: Eugen
items.unlockOrAccess: Available through the base story roster.
items.bestFor: Players who want to target weak points from range and control space without constantly standing under a boss.

items.name: Rosetta
items.unlockOrAccess: Available through the base story roster.
items.bestFor: Players who enjoy positioning tools, area control, and supportive damage patterns.

items.name: Lancelot
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who like quick combos, evasive play, and aggressive melee pressure.

items.name: Vane
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who want a sturdy frontline fighter with party-saving defensive value.

items.name: Percival
items.role: Fire-aligned sword attacker with charged strikes
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who enjoy deliberate melee timing, strong hit confirmation, and stylish burst damage.

items.name: Siegfried
items.role: Heavy sword fighter built around timing
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who want powerful melee strings and are comfortable learning rhythm-based inputs.

items.name: Charlotta
items.role: Compact melee attacker with rapid sword combos
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who like fast attacks, close-range pressure, and a small, mobile target profile.

items.name: Yodarha
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who enjoy counters, sharp timing, and compact melee skill expression.

items.name: Narmaya
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who want a high-ceiling melee character with stance flow and burst potential.

items.name: Zeta
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who like jump-based offense, spear combos, and staying active above ground threats.

items.name: Vaseraga
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who prefer slow, heavy hits, armor-style commitment, and huge melee impact.

items.name: Ferry
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who want mid-range control, summon-like pressure, and a different rhythm from standard sword users.

items.name: Ghandagoza
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who enjoy close-range brawling, big punches, and simple-looking attacks with strict timing.

items.name: Cagliostro
items.role: Alchemist with damage, healing, and utility
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked through crewmate access in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who want a flexible caster-support hybrid with strong team usefulness.

items.name: Id
items.unlockOrAccess: Available after progressing through the base game story.
items.bestFor: Players who want a dramatic melee kit tied closely to Relink's main story.

items.name: Seofon
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked at no additional cost by meeting in-game requirements, or unlocked immediately through a paid character unlock set.
items.bestFor: Players who want a late-game sword character and enjoy unlocking powerful roster additions through challenge content.

items.name: Tweyen
items.unlockOrAccess: Unlocked at no additional cost by meeting in-game requirements, or unlocked immediately through a paid character unlock set.
items.bestFor: Players who want a precision ranged character with endgame-style unlock progression.

items.name: Sandalphon
items.role: Winged sword fighter with late-game story importance
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as a playable character in Version 1.3.0, with access tied to update content and related unlock items.
items.bestFor: Players who want a major Granblue character added after launch and are ready for higher-level postgame content.

items.name: Gallanza
items.role: Heavy spear-axe powerhouse with wide attack reach
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who want broad melee swings, a heavyweight feel, and a former Avia commander on their side.

items.name: Maglielle
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who like elegant ranged-melee blade control, flashy attacks, and a stylish boss-turned-playable pick.

items.name: Beatrix
items.role: Society attacker with offense, defense, and recovery buff cycles
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who want an aggressive sword character with stance-like buff management and a high-energy combat style.

items.name: Eustace
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who prefer composed ranged combat, tactical spacing, and a firearm-based kit.

items.name: Fraux
items.role: Pactbearer to The Devil with striking power
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who want an Evoker-style character with forceful close-range identity.

items.name: Fediel
items.role: Darkness-aligned dragon of the Six Dragons
items.unlockOrAccess: Added as one of the six playable characters in the Endless Ragnarok upgrade.
items.bestFor: Players who want a powerful dark-element fantasy, dragon lore, and a standout expansion character.

items.name: Lyria
items.unlockOrAccess: Appears throughout the base game story rather than as a standard quest roster pick.
items.bestFor: Players following the main story and Granblue lore.

items.name: Vyrn
items.unlockOrAccess: Appears throughout the base game story.
items.bestFor: Players tracking the Grandcypher crew and story dialogue.

items.name: Rolan
items.unlockOrAccess: Appears as a story-focused character in the base game.
items.bestFor: Players who care about Relink's original story arc.

items.name: Lilith
items.role: Leader of the Pilgrims of Avia
items.unlockOrAccess: Encountered through the base game narrative.
items.bestFor: Players following the Avia conflict and campaign bosses.

items.name: Lucilius
items.role: High-difficulty boss known as the Primal Progenitor
items.unlockOrAccess: Fought through the post-story quest The Final Vision and related high-difficulty content.
items.bestFor: Players looking for the signature late-game raid-style challenge.

items.name: Beelzebub
items.role: Major expansion boss known as the Eternity Ender
items.unlockOrAccess: Appears as part of the Endless Ragnarok expansion content.
items.bestFor: Players seeking expansion-era boss encounters and harder postgame progression.

items.name: The World
items.role: Arcarum-linked boss known as the Inchoate World
items.unlockOrAccess: Appears as part of the Endless Ragnarok expansion content.
items.bestFor: Players interested in the expansion's Arcarum-themed threats and boss roster.

granblueRelinkBuildsWeaponsSigils.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Builds, Weapons, and Sigils
granblueRelinkBuildsWeaponsSigils.subtitle: Builds, weapons, sigils, Damage Cap, and Endless Ragnarok progression in one table.
granblueRelinkBuildsWeaponsSigils.intro: Use this progression table to understand what to build first, when sigils start to matter, and how late-game systems such as Sigil Synthesis and Master Traits connect to character growth.

items.buildGoal: Learn one character, keep the party alive, and upgrade weapons steadily instead of chasing perfect sigils early.
items.weapons: Use the best available weapon for your current character and upgrade it when materials are easy to spare. Base attack and weapon level matter more than rare optimization at this stage.
items.sigils: Equip simple attack, health, defense, cooldown, and utility sigils that match how you actually play. Do not waste rare materials trying to force a final build before endgame.
items.keyTerms: Character level, Mastery, weapon level, skills, basic sigils
items.playerTip: Pick a main character and three AI party members you like. Spreading every upgrade across the whole roster too early slows progress.

items.buildGoal: Start shaping characters by role: damage dealer, ranged attacker, support, tanky frontline, or utility pick.
items.weapons: Continue strengthening the weapons used by your active party. Match upgrades to characters you actually bring into quests.
items.sigils: Begin replacing filler sigils with stronger damage, survivability, cooldown, and character-friendly choices. Keep practical defensive sigils when learning harder fights.
items.keyTerms: Quest Counter, role builds, skill loadouts, party balance
items.playerTip: Keep enough survivability to maintain damage uptime and clear quests consistently.

items.buildGoal: Move from general power to focused damage uptime, boss control, and stable clears.
items.weapons: Invest more heavily in the weapons of your main party and begin planning around endgame weapon growth instead of constantly swapping favorites.
items.sigils: Start prioritizing Damage Cap when your attacks begin reaching the game's damage ceiling. Combine it with offensive sigils, useful utility traits, and survival tools for hard quests.
items.keyTerms: Damage Cap, boss uptime, Link Attacks, Skybound Arts, Chain Bursts
items.playerTip: When more raw attack stops raising real hit numbers, Damage Cap becomes a core build piece rather than a luxury.

items.buildGoal: Build around stable high-difficulty clears, not just attractive stat screens.
items.weapons: Push your main weapon path hard, then support it with sigils that let you stay active through dangerous boss patterns.
items.sigils: Damage Cap, War Elemental, Supplementary Damage, cooldown tools, quick skill access, and defensive insurance become high-value priorities for endgame-ready builds.
items.keyTerms: War Elemental, Supplementary Damage V, Damage Cap, Lucilius, The Final Vision
items.playerTip: War Elemental and Supplementary Damage are premium endgame-style sigils. Treat them as build accelerators, not as a reason to ignore core weapon and mastery growth.

items.buildGoal: Refine endgame builds by combining sigils and traits once your account has enough strong legendary sigils.
items.weapons: Keep weapon upgrades moving while using synthesis to reduce awkward trait gaps in your build.
items.sigils: Sigil Synthesis lets players combine sigils and traits, including eligible legendary sigils and sigils marked for synthesis, to create stronger build pieces.
items.keyTerms: Sigil Synthesis, legendary sigils, traits, Version 1.3.0
items.playerTip: Use synthesis to solve specific trait gaps and improve established endgame builds.

items.buildGoal: Build Seofon, Tweyen, and Sandalphon after unlocking them through update content or unlock items.
items.weapons: Treat each update character as a serious build project with their own weapon, mastery, skill, and sigil needs.
items.sigils: Use the same progression logic: safe early setup, focused damage and utility later, then endgame traits and Damage Cap when their numbers justify it.
items.keyTerms: Seofon, Tweyen, Sandalphon, character unlock sets, update quests
items.playerTip: New characters still need full investment. Unlocking them is not the same thing as making them ready for high-difficulty quests.

items.buildGoal: Use Master Traits, summons, new co-op tiers, and Chaos difficulty to push builds beyond the base postgame.
items.weapons: Continue enhancing characters and weapons with materials earned from expansion quests and Conflux runs.
items.sigils: Keep core endgame sigils while using Master Traits and expansion rewards to specialize characters further.
items.keyTerms: Master Traits, summons, Chaos difficulty, Ragnalia, Beelzebub, The World
items.playerTip: Master Traits are an expansion-layer growth system. Build them alongside sigils instead of treating them as replacements.

items.buildGoal: Create self-sufficient builds that can clear sequential solo zones and adapt to temporary perks.
items.weapons: Bring characters with upgraded weapons and reliable personal damage because Conflux progress depends on your solo execution.
items.sigils: Favor consistent damage, survival, and utility. Temporary perks can enhance a run, but the base build still needs to stand on its own.
items.keyTerms: Conflux, temporary perks, rare materials, high-tier equipment, solo mode
items.playerTip: Conflux rewards flexible builds. Over-specialized setups can crumble when a run asks for objectives your build cannot handle.

granblueRelinkQuestsBosses.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Quests and Bosses
granblueRelinkQuestsBosses.subtitle: Quest order, update bosses, Chaos difficulty, and Conflux progression on one path.
granblueRelinkQuestsBosses.intro: Follow the content path from main story quests into postgame, Lucilius, free update quests, Endless Ragnarok's Chaos difficulty, and the Conflux solo mode.

items.title: Main story and standard quest counter
items.summary: Start with campaign progress, character access, basic party building, and regular Quest Counter missions.
items.content: The base game teaches core battle systems such as normal attacks, skills, Link Attacks, Skybound Arts, Chain Bursts, dodges, guarding, and four-character party play. After the story opens up more quest access, the Quest Counter becomes the main place to farm materials, raise characters, and prepare for harder postgame content.
items.bossesOrTargets: Campaign bosses, standard Quest Counter targets, material quests
items.recommendedOrder: Play before all update and expansion content.

items.title: Post-story and Proud preparation
items.summary: Farm materials, strengthen weapons, build sigils, and prepare your core party for the hardest base-game quests.
items.content: Post-story progression shifts from simply clearing quests to making your main team reliable. Focus on weapon upgrades, Mastery, useful sigils, skill loadouts, and party balance. This is the stage where boss mechanics matter more and incomplete builds start getting punished.
items.bossesOrTargets: Higher-difficulty Quest Counter encounters and Proud-level preparation targets
items.recommendedOrder: Clear after the story and before Lucilius or expansion-era challenge tiers.

items.title: The Final Vision and Lucilius
items.summary: Version 1.1.0 adds The Final Vision, a post-story quest featuring Lucilius.
items.content: The Final Vision becomes available after completing a story segment in Seedhollow. It is built as a high-difficulty update quest and functions as one of the major late-game boss checks before later update and expansion progression.
items.recommendedOrder: Attempt after your party has strong weapons, practical sigils, and reliable survival.

items.title: Seofon and Tweyen unlock quests
items.summary: Version 1.2.0 adds Seofon and Tweyen as playable characters and adds quests tied to their update content.
items.content: The update introduces the quests Lock Horns and Gulp... So These Are the Rumored Monsters, alongside Seofon and Tweyen as playable roster additions. Both characters can be unlocked at no additional cost by meeting in-game requirements, with paid unlock sets available for immediate access.
items.bossesOrTargets: Lock Horns, Gulp... So These Are the Rumored Monsters
items.recommendedOrder: Clear after building a stable postgame team, then invest in the new characters if you want to main them.

items.title: Sandalphon, Zero, and Version 1.3 challenge quests
items.summary: Version 1.3.0 adds Sandalphon, new quests, Photo Mode, Sigil Synthesis, and additional endgame systems.
items.content: The update adds Sandalphon as a playable character and introduces Pageant of Might and Magic, Zathba's Volunteers, Dark Swordsman's Nightmare, and Zero. It also adds Sigil Synthesis, giving endgame players a deeper way to combine sigils and traits.
items.bossesOrTargets: Pageant of Might and Magic, Zathba's Volunteers, Dark Swordsman's Nightmare, Zero
items.recommendedOrder: Play after Version 1.1 and Version 1.2 content when your team is ready for deeper postgame optimization.

items.title: Endless Ragnarok story and Chaos quests
items.summary: Endless Ragnarok adds a new story arc, new co-op quest tiers, Chaos difficulty, Master Traits, summons, and major bosses.
items.content: Endless Ragnarok expands the game with new stories and game modes, including the domain of Ragnalia, gateways, Beelzebub, new co-op quest tiers, summon unlocks, and Master Traits. Chaos difficulty is presented as the expansion's most challenging quest tier, with bosses gaining EX Burst attacks.
items.bossesOrTargets: Ragnalia, Beelzebub, The World, Chaos-tier bosses
items.recommendedOrder: Start after clearing the base game and building at least one serious endgame party.

items.title: Summons and Primal Bursts
items.summary: Endless Ragnarok adds summons that provide attack skills, support abilities, and Primal Burst follow-ups.
items.content: Summons can be equipped to characters and used in battle for offensive, support, or crowd-control value. Some summons can be invoked and controlled for a short period, and certain conditions allow Primal Bursts from powers such as Proto Bahamut and Excavallion.
items.bossesOrTargets: Summon unlock quests, Primal Burst-enabled encounters
items.recommendedOrder: Use throughout Endless Ragnarok progression once summon access opens.

items.title: The Conflux solo endgame
items.summary: The Conflux is a solo mode with sequential zones, objectives, battles, temporary perks, and high-tier rewards.
items.content: Conflux runs send one character through multiple zones with puzzles, battles, and special traits or buffs. Each zone can offer temporary perk choices, while rewards include treasure used to enhance characters and weapons, rare materials, and high-tier equipment.
items.bossesOrTargets: Solo Conflux objectives, zone bosses, reward encounters
items.recommendedOrder: Enter after you have an expansion-ready character with upgraded weapons, practical sigils, and strong personal survivability.

granblueRelinkPlatformsCrossplay.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Platforms and Crossplay
granblueRelinkPlatformsCrossplay.subtitle: Platforms, multiplayer, crossplay, Switch 2, and PC requirements compared.
granblueRelinkPlatformsCrossplay.intro: Compare PlayStation, Steam, and Nintendo Switch 2 support before buying the base game or Endless Ragnarok. This table separates base-game platform rules from the expansion's all-platform crossplay support.

items.version: Base game and Endless Ragnarok support
items.players: 1 player offline; online co-op supports 1-4 players.
items.crossplay: Base game cross-platform play is supported between PS4 and PS5. Endless Ragnarok supports crossplay with Nintendo Switch 2, PS5, PS4, and Steam.
items.requirementsOrNotes: The PS4 version can be upgraded to the digital PS5 version at no additional cost. Online play requires the relevant platform online service.
items.bestFor: Players staying in the PlayStation ecosystem or upgrading from PS4 to PS5.

items.version: Base game and Endless Ragnarok support
items.players: 1 player offline; online co-op supports 1-4 players.
items.crossplay: Base game cross-platform play is supported between PS5 and PS4. Endless Ragnarok supports crossplay with Nintendo Switch 2, PS5, PS4, and Steam.
items.requirementsOrNotes: PlayStation 5 is one of the official launch platforms for the base game and one of the supported platforms for Endless Ragnarok.
items.bestFor: Players who want console play, PlayStation cross-generation co-op, and expansion crossplay.

items.players: Single-player and online co-op for up to four players.
items.crossplay: The base game does not support cross-platform play with PlayStation. Base-game cross-platform play is listed only for PS4 and PS5.
items.requirementsOrNotes: The Steam base game is required before purchasing and using Endless Ragnarok upgrade content on Steam.
items.bestFor: PC players who want keyboard and controller flexibility and Steam multiplayer.

items.topic: Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit on Steam
items.version: Standard Edition or Special Edition upgrade kit
items.players: Online co-op supports up to four players in supported expansion quests.
items.crossplay: Endless Ragnarok supports four-player online co-op crossplay across Nintendo Switch 2, PS5, PS4, and Steam.
items.requirementsOrNotes: The upgrade kit requires the base game on the same platform. The Special Edition includes extra color packs, useful item packs, and character unlock-related items for Seofon, Tweyen, and Sandalphon.
items.bestFor: Existing Steam owners who want the expansion story, Chaos quests, Conflux, summons, Master Traits, and new playable characters.

items.players: Supports handheld, tabletop, and TV modes. Online play is supported, and local communication supports up to four-player co-op.
items.crossplay: Endless Ragnarok supports crossplay with Nintendo Switch 2, PS5, PS4, and Steam.
items.requirementsOrNotes: Nintendo lists the release date as July 9, 2026, download size as 38.7 GB, and supported languages including Japanese, English, Spanish, French, German, Italian, Portuguese, Korean, and Chinese. Save Data Cloud backup is listed as unsupported.
items.bestFor: Players who want portable play, local Switch 2 co-op, or all-platform expansion crossplay.

items.players: Online co-op supports up to four players where online play is available.
items.crossplay: Base game: PS4 and PS5 crossplay only. Endless Ragnarok: Nintendo Switch 2, PS5, PS4, and Steam crossplay.
items.requirementsOrNotes: Platform membership or online service requirements may apply. GameShare is not available during Nintendo Switch 2 Local Communication sessions.
items.bestFor: Players buying the game mainly to co-op with friends on different platforms.

items.version: Base game and Endless Ragnarok PC requirement profile
items.players: Online co-op support depends on Steam and network access.
items.crossplay: Base Steam does not crossplay with PlayStation; Endless Ragnarok supports expansion crossplay across all listed platforms.
items.requirementsOrNotes: Minimum: Windows 11 64-bit, Intel Core i3-9100 or AMD Ryzen 3 3200G, 16 GB RAM, NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6 GB or AMD Radeon RX 580 8 GB, DirectX 11, 90 GB available storage, SSD recommended, target profile 1080p and 30 FPS on Standard settings.
items.bestFor: PC players checking whether their system can run the game before buying.

items.version: Base game and Endless Ragnarok PC requirement profile
items.players: Online co-op support depends on Steam and network access.
items.crossplay: Base Steam does not crossplay with PlayStation; Endless Ragnarok supports expansion crossplay across all listed platforms.
items.requirementsOrNotes: Recommended: Windows 11 64-bit, Intel Core i7-8700 or AMD Ryzen 5 3600, 16 GB RAM, NVIDIA GeForce RTX 2080 8 GB or AMD Radeon RX 6700 XT 12 GB, DirectX 11, 90 GB available storage, SSD recommended, target profile 1080p and 60 FPS on Ultra settings.
items.bestFor: PC players who want smoother high-setting gameplay and a safer setup for expansion co-op.

faq.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink
faq.subtitle: Key questions about Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok before you play.

questions.question: What is Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok?
questions.answer: Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok is the major expansion for Granblue Fantasy: Relink. It adds a new story chapter, six playable characters, Chaos quests, the Conflux solo mode, Summons, and cross-platform online play across Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam.

questions.question: When does Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok release?
questions.answer: Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok launches on July 9, 2026 according to Cygames. The Endless Ragnarok demo became available on June 18, 2026 on PlayStation ahead of the full release.

questions.question: Does Granblue Fantasy Relink use gift codes?
questions.answer: Granblue Fantasy Relink does not use public universal gift codes. Real rewards come from the Relink Pack for eligible PlayStation copies, official pre-order and early purchase bonuses, and Endless Ragnarok edition bonuses. Avoid third-party code lists and code generators that ask for account details.

questions.question: Which platforms is Granblue Fantasy Relink available on?
questions.answer: Granblue Fantasy Relink is available on PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam. The Endless Ragnarok expansion also adds Nintendo Switch 2, with cross-platform online play across all supported platforms for 1 to 4 players.

questions.question: Do I need the base game to play Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok?
questions.answer: Existing Granblue Fantasy Relink owners can buy the Endless Ragnarok Upgrade Kit, which requires the base game on the same platform. New players can buy a full Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok edition that includes the base game and the expansion together.

questions.question: Is Granblue Fantasy Relink singleplayer or co-op?
questions.answer: Granblue Fantasy Relink supports 1 player offline and 1 to 4 players online. You can play the full story and quests solo with AI teammates, or join online co-op with other players, and Endless Ragnarok adds crossplay so PlayStation, Steam, and Nintendo Switch 2 users can play together.

cta.title: Ready to Play Granblue Fantasy: Relink?
cta.description: Join the Granblue Fantasy: Relink community on Discord and official channels for guides, builds, and Endless Ragnarok updates!

expansion.metaDescription: Everything about the Granblue Fantasy Relink Endless Ragnarok expansion: release date, editions, Upgrade Kit, demo, new characters, Chaos quests, and crossplay.
expansion.whatIsDescription: Endless Ragnarok is the major expansion for Granblue Fantasy: Relink. It adds a new story chapter, six playable characters, the Conflux solo mode, Summons, Master Traits, Chaos difficulty, and cross-platform online play across Nintendo Switch 2, PlayStation 5, PlayStation 4, and Steam.

whySteps.step2Description: Check the launch date, demo rewards, and early purchase bonus windows for your platform.
whySteps.step3Description: Get ready for new characters, Chaos quests, Conflux, summons, and all-platform crossplay.

characters.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Characters
characters.description: Every playable character in Granblue Fantasy Relink across the base roster, free updates, and the Endless Ragnarok expansion.
characters.metaTitle: Granblue Fantasy Relink Characters - Base Roster, Updates & Expansion
characters.metaDescription: Browse all Granblue Fantasy Relink characters including base roster, free update additions, and Endless Ragnarok newcomers with roles and unlock notes.
characters.whatIsDescription: Granblue Fantasy Relink features a large playable roster built from the base game, free post-launch updates, and the six newcomers added in Endless Ragnarok. Each character has a distinct weapon, role, and unlock path.

whySteps.step1Description: Compare roles, weapons, and playstyles to pick a character worth building first.
whySteps.step2Description: See which characters come from the story, free updates, or the Endless Ragnarok expansion.
whySteps.step3Description: Match a main damage dealer with ranged, defensive, and support teammates for any quest.

tier.metaTitle: Granblue Fantasy Relink Tier List - Best Characters for 2026
tier.metaDescription: Rank every Granblue Fantasy Relink character by beginner ease, AI value, co-op utility, late-game damage, and survivability in this 2026 tier list.
tier.whatIsDescription: Cygames does not publish an official strength ranking. This site editorial tier list ranks Granblue Fantasy Relink characters by beginner comfort, AI teammate value, co-op usefulness, late-game damage, and survivability, so you can choose who to build first.

whySteps.step1Description: Pick S-tier characters that stay valuable from the story into the endgame.
whySteps.step2Description: See which characters perform reliably when controlled by the AI in solo play.
whySteps.step3Description: Find characters that shine in online quests and high-difficulty expansion content.

builds.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Builds
builds.description: Granblue Fantasy Relink builds, weapons, sigils, team comps, and progression from the story into Damage Cap, War Elemental, and Endless Ragnarok endgame.
builds.metaTitle: Granblue Fantasy Relink Builds - Weapons, Sigils & Best Team
builds.metaDescription: Build Granblue Fantasy Relink characters and teams with weapons, sigils, Damage Cap, War Elemental, and Endless Ragnarok progression guidance.
builds.whatIsDescription: Builds in Granblue Fantasy Relink combine weapons, Mastery trees, skills, and sigils into a focused loadout for each character. Strong builds balance damage, survivability, cooldowns, and party synergy from the story into endgame content.

whySteps.step1Description: Move from simple early upgrades to focused late-game damage and Damage Cap setups.
whySteps.step2Description: Avoid wasting rare materials on every weapon and prioritize your main party first.
whySteps.step3Description: Use War Elemental, Supplementary Damage, Sigil Synthesis, and Master Traits for expansion content.

sigils.title: Granblue Fantasy Relink Sigils
sigils.description: Granblue Fantasy Relink sigil system explained: types, Damage Cap, War Elemental, Supplementary Damage, Sigil Synthesis, and Master Traits.
sigils.metaTitle: Granblue Fantasy Relink Sigils - Damage Cap, War Elemental & Synthesis
sigils.metaDescription: Master Granblue Fantasy Relink sigils including Damage Cap, War Elemental, Supplementary Damage, Sigil Synthesis, and Master Traits for endgame builds.
sigils.whatIsDescription: Sigils are the core customization system in Granblue Fantasy Relink. Each character equips sigils that shape attack, defense, utility, cooldowns, and character-specific traits. Late-game sigils such as Damage Cap, War Elemental, and Supplementary Damage define endgame builds.

whySteps.step1Description: Learn how sigil slots, levels, and traits work before investing rare materials.
whySteps.step2Description: Find Damage Cap, War Elemental, and Supplementary Damage to push real damage numbers.
whySteps.step3Description: Combine sigils and traits to close gaps in established endgame builds.
